title: Lesson v1
description:
A captured lesson from a security research / CTF / bounty / CVE session.
Per roadmap section 8.2.

MANDATORY `source_kind` and `trust` fields (per handoff section 5
contradiction 'Lesson schema omits mandatory source_kind' and per
SI-003 trust policy):
  - source_kind controls how the lesson may be used (indexing, prime,
    promotion). It is the policy gate.
  - trust mirrors source_kind into the gbrain trust filter vocabulary
    (never-prime | always-prime | workflow | external). It is the
    runtime filter hook.
Both are required so a lesson cannot enter the brain without an
explicit trust decision.

lesson_id.description:
Stable identifier for this lesson. Convention: 'lesson-<uuid>' or
'lesson-<engagement>-<slug>'. Must be unique within the brain.

claim.description:
The claim this lesson makes, in one sentence. Should be falsifiable.
E.g. 'H1 Report Assistant approval is not vulnerability ground truth'.
examples: H1 Report Assistant approval is not vulnerability ground truth

kind.description:
What kind of lesson this is.
- observation: a fact noticed during work.
- heuristic: a rule-of-thumb that worked.
- pattern: a recurring structure across engagements.
- pitfall: a mistake to avoid.
- fix: a fix that worked.

status.description:
Lifecycle state of the lesson (roadmap section 8.1):
candidate -> verified -> replicated -> promotion candidate ->
evaluated -> canary -> stable -> superseded/retracted.
'rejected' is a terminal state for lessons that failed validation.

source_kind.description:
Origin classification per SI-003 trust policy. Controls how the
lesson may be used (indexing, prime, promotion). MANDATORY — a lesson
without a source_kind MUST be rejected by the curator.

trust.description:
gbrain trust filter vocabulary. Mirrors source_kind for the runtime
filter. MANDATORY — a lesson without a trust label MUST be rejected.
- never-prime: never surfaced in prime (target_derived).
- always-prime: safe to surface in prime (public, verified external).
- workflow: workflow-internal, surfaced only in same-engagement contexts.
- external: from an external source, surfaced with a warning until verified.

captured_at.description: ISO 8601 UTC timestamp the lesson was captured.

source.description:
Where the lesson was derived from. null when the lesson is fully
abstract (no specific engagement/workspace/artifact).

artifact.description: Relative path to the artifact that grounded the lesson.

artifact_sha256.description: SHA256 of the artifact (for integrity / replay).

platform_outcome.description: Platform outcome state that grounded the lesson, when applicable.

applicability.description:
Where this lesson applies. Used by the curator to decide whether to
surface the lesson in a given context.

technologies.description:
Technology stack the lesson applies to (e.g. ['notion', 'web']).
Empty array means 'applies broadly'.

engagement_types.description:
Engagement types the lesson applies to (e.g. ['bounty', 'ctf']).
Empty array means 'applies to all engagement types'.

preconditions.description:
Conditions that must hold for the lesson to apply. Free-form strings.
Empty array means 'no preconditions'.

evidence.description:
Evidence supporting the lesson's claim. Each item is a (claim, support)
pair. Optional — a candidate lesson may have no evidence yet.

examples: Endpoint returns workspace metadata without authentication

support.description: How the sub-claim is supported (artifact path, quote, hash, etc.).

promoted_to.description:
Path to the skill file this lesson was promoted to, when status='promoted'.
null when not promoted.

superseded_by.description: lesson_id of a newer lesson that replaces this one. null when not superseded.

examples.claim: H1 Report Assistant approval is not vulnerability ground truth
